_meta.purpose: Lookup table for diagnosing FDM print defects from a webcam photo and mapping them to concrete OrcaSlicer setting changes.

defects.name: Stringing / oozing between parts
defects.visual_id: thin hair-like plastic strands between separate features or towers
causes: Nozzle temperature too high, filament too runny while traveling
Retraction distance/speed insufficient for the extruder type
Travel moves crossing open space unnecessarily

orca_settings.note: so retraction triggers on shorter moves

hardware_fixes: check for worn/leaking nozzle if strings persist despite tuning

defects.name: Warping / lifting off the bed (corner curling)
defects.visual_id: corners peeling upward, print detached at edges, base curled
causes: Uneven cooling causing differential shrinkage (worse ABS/ASA, large flat parts)
Insufficient bed adhesion/temperature or poor first-layer squish
Drafts / ambient cooling on one side

orca_settings.note: 5-10mm+ for problem geometries, or use a raft
only if base is also over-squished

defects.name: Layer shifting (X or Y offset mid-print)
defects.visual_id: clean horizontal offset/step in an otherwise vertical wall, all layers above shift consistently
causes: Belt slipping / loose or stretched
Print speed or acceleration too high causing motor to skip steps
Loose pulley grub screws or motor driver overheating/undercurrent

orca_settings.note: allows higher speeds safely once hardware confirmed OK

defects.name: Elephant's foot (bulging first layers)
defects.visual_id: base flares outward wider than the rest of the part, bulge in bottom 1-3 layers
causes: Nozzle/Z-offset too low, over-squishing first layer
Bed temp too hot keeping bottom layers soft under weight above
Insufficient first-layer cooling

orca_settings.note: start 80-90% width reduction (~0.1-0.2mm inset) over 1-2 layers

hardware_fixes: re-level bed / recalibrate Z-offset (raise in +0.02-0.05mm increments)

defects.name: Poor first layer adhesion / not sticking
defects.visual_id: print detaches entirely from bed, corners lift immediately, spaghetti on bed
causes: Bed not level or nozzle too far from bed
Bed surface dirty/greasy or temperature too low
First layer print speed too fast

defects.name: Under-extrusion (gaps, weak layers, thin walls)
defects.visual_id: visible gaps between extrusion lines, thin/see-through walls, missing material streaks
causes: Nozzle partially clogged or temp too low for flow rate
Flow/extrusion multiplier miscalibrated (under 100%)
Wet filament or incorrect filament diameter setting

orca_settings.note: only if walls are structurally too thin, separate from flow issue

hardware_fixes: check PTFE tube seating at hotend

defects.name: Over-extrusion (blobs, dimensional inaccuracy)
defects.visual_id: oversized/bulging walls, blobby texture, parts too big vs tolerances
causes: Flow ratio/extrusion multiplier too high (>100%)
Nozzle temp too high causing excess ooze/priming
Incorrect filament diameter setting
hardware_fixes: verify filament diameter with calipers at multiple points

defects.name: Ghosting / ringing (wavy surface artifacts)
defects.visual_id: repeating wavy ripples on vertical walls near corners/holes, echo pattern after sharp direction change
causes: Excess print-head momentum/vibration from high acceleration/jerk
Loose frame, belts, or gantry causing mechanical resonance
Print speed too high for the machine's rigidity

defects.name: Gaps or holes in top surface / poor top infill
defects.visual_id: small pinholes or sagging striped pattern on top flat surface, infill pattern shadow showing through
causes: Too few top solid layers to bridge over sparse infill gaps
Sparse infill density/pattern too open, top layer sags into gaps
Under-extrusion or poor cooling on top layers

orca_settings.note: switch to gyroid/grid, better top-layer support than lines
near 100% during top solid infill for PLA

hardware_fixes: verify nozzle isn't partially clogged if issue also appears on walls

defects.name: Curling/lifting on overhangs, sagging bridges
defects.visual_id: drooping strands or wavy sag under unsupported horizontal spans, rough underside on angled overhangs
causes: Insufficient cooling, filament stays molten too long over open air
Print speed too fast for bridge to set before next line
No support generated for steep overhang angles

orca_settings.note: near 100% PLA/PETG, keep low for ABS/ASA
e.g. 45deg so supports generate for steeper overhangs
so cooling kicks in earlier on shallower overhangs

hardware_fixes: ensure part-cooling fan/duct isn't obstructed or misdirected

defects.name: Nozzle clogs / clicking extruder (skipped steps)
defects.visual_id: no visible defect until under/no-extrusion appears; audibly diagnosed (repetitive clicking/grinding from extruder)
causes: Partial/full nozzle clog (debris, burnt filament, moisture bubbles)
Nozzle temp too low for material/speed combo
Extruder tension too tight (grinding filament) or too loose

orca_settings.note: temporarily, if forcing material through partial clog

hardware_fixes: run cleaning wire through nozzle at ~250C

defects.name: Cracking/splitting along layer lines (esp. ABS/ASA)
defects.visual_id: visible split/delamination between layers, part breaks cleanly along a horizontal layer plane
causes: Insufficient interlayer bonding from rapid/uneven cooling (thermal stress)
Nozzle temp too low for full layer fusion
Cooling fan running when it shouldn't (drafts on ABS/ASA)
hardware_fixes: eliminate cooling fan drafts entirely for ABS/ASA

defects.name: Rough/blobby seams (Z-seam visibility)
defects.visual_id: vertical line of small bumps/blobs running up one side of an otherwise smooth wall
causes: Retraction/priming imbalance leaving extra material at layer start/stop point
Seam placement algorithm choosing a visually exposed location
Print speed too fast at seam transition

orca_settings.note: "nearest" or "aligned" to hide seam in existing geometry
offset internal wall seams from outer seam

hardware_fixes: if blobs occur every layer regardless of settings, check for nozzle drool from heat creep

defects.name: Warping specific to ABS/ASA (enclosure-related)
defects.visual_id: large flat areas curling/lifting at edges, cracking near base, most severe on tall/wide ABS parts printed in open air
causes: No enclosure, rapid uneven cooling causes internal stress and shrinkage
Bed temp/chamber temp too low to keep part warm through the print
Cooling fan active when it should be off/minimal

orca_settings.note: near 0%, except overhang_fan_speed where geometry demands it
10mm+ or use a raft for large flat parts

hardware_fixes: build/use a heated or passive enclosure
eliminate direct airflow (AC vents, open windows) near printer
